markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the official vote count is certified.
markets.rules_primary: If the total vote count in the first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary is 3200000, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The vote count refers to the officially certified results from the relevant election authority. Provisional, absentee, early, mail-in, or electronic ballots are included only if reflected in the official certification. In preferential or ranked-choice systems, the contract resolves based on the vote count identified as the official result by the certifying authority. If the election is held in multiple phases, the contract resolves on the certified aggregate total. If a runoff forms part of the certified result, it is encompassed. The contract resolves based on officially certified results regardless of ongoing appeals. Preliminary counts, exit polls, projections, or uncertified results do not qualify. If the election is postponed but held within one year of the originally scheduled date, the contract remains valid; if postponed beyond one year or canceled, all contracts resolve to No.
markets.title: Will the total vote count for total votes in first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary be above 3200000?

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the official vote count is certified.
markets.rules_primary: If the total vote count in the first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary is 3100000, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The vote count refers to the officially certified results from the relevant election authority. Provisional, absentee, early, mail-in, or electronic ballots are included only if reflected in the official certification. In preferential or ranked-choice systems, the contract resolves based on the vote count identified as the official result by the certifying authority. If the election is held in multiple phases, the contract resolves on the certified aggregate total. If a runoff forms part of the certified result, it is encompassed. The contract resolves based on officially certified results regardless of ongoing appeals. Preliminary counts, exit polls, projections, or uncertified results do not qualify. If the election is postponed but held within one year of the originally scheduled date, the contract remains valid; if postponed beyond one year or canceled, all contracts resolve to No.

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the official vote count is certified.
markets.rules_primary: If the total vote count in the first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary is 3000000, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The vote count refers to the officially certified results from the relevant election authority. Provisional, absentee, early, mail-in, or electronic ballots are included only if reflected in the official certification. In preferential or ranked-choice systems, the contract resolves based on the vote count identified as the official result by the certifying authority. If the election is held in multiple phases, the contract resolves on the certified aggregate total. If a runoff forms part of the certified result, it is encompassed. The contract resolves based on officially certified results regardless of ongoing appeals. Preliminary counts, exit polls, projections, or uncertified results do not qualify. If the election is postponed but held within one year of the originally scheduled date, the contract remains valid; if postponed beyond one year or canceled, all contracts resolve to No.
markets.title: Will the total vote count for total votes in first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary be above 3000000?

markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the official vote count is certified.
markets.rules_primary: If the total vote count in the first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary is 2900000, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The vote count refers to the officially certified results from the relevant election authority. Provisional, absentee, early, mail-in, or electronic ballots are included only if reflected in the official certification. In preferential or ranked-choice systems, the contract resolves based on the vote count identified as the official result by the certifying authority. If the election is held in multiple phases, the contract resolves on the certified aggregate total. If a runoff forms part of the certified result, it is encompassed. The contract resolves based on officially certified results regardless of ongoing appeals. Preliminary counts, exit polls, projections, or uncertified results do not qualify. If the election is postponed but held within one year of the originally scheduled date, the contract remains valid; if postponed beyond one year or canceled, all contracts resolve to No.
markets.title: Will the total vote count for total votes in first round of the 2026 Texas Senate Democratic primary be above 2900000?
